title: zwingera aruba
intro: Zwingera aruba, commonly known as Pterocarpus marsupium or Indian cedar, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving heart health, and lowering blood sugar levels. The therapeutic actions of Zwingera aruba include acting as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective agent, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Zwingera aruba can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its high alkaloid content. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant's extract can lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as increased heart rate and blood pressure. In severe cases, improper use of the zwingera aruba plant can cause seizures, vomiting, and even respiratory failure. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using the plant for any health benefits to avoid these potential side effects.
